Based on the search results, I found relevant information about cloudflared websocket and buffering behavior, though no results specifically mention version 2026.5.0.

The most relevant information comes from Cloudflare's official troubleshooting documentation: Proxied traffic through Cloudflare Tunnel is buffered by default unless the origin server includes the Content-Type: text/event-stream response header, which tells cloudflared to stream data as it arrives instead of buffering the entire response.

Additionally, the search results show several documented issues related to websocket performance with cloudflared:

- WebSockets are slow to connect when using cloudflared, with some users reporting it takes over 8 seconds to setup a websocket connection through tunnels.

- Buffer size issues are reported by the quic-go library leveraged by cloudflared, and can be manually overridden for testing purposes in high-bandwidth environments.

The search results do not contain any specific release notes or documentation for cloudflared version 2026.5.0 regarding websocket buffering, streaming, or latency improvements. If you're looking for release notes for that specific version, you may want to check the [official cloudflared GitHub repository](https://github.com/cloudflare/cloudflared/releases) directly.

Links: [{"title":"Server-sent-events are buffered. · Issue #199 · cloudflare/cloudflared","url":"https://github.com/cloudflare/cloudflared/issues/199"},{"title":"🐛 SSE over GET is not streamed in real-time on Quick Tunnel — all data is flushed only after the server closes the connection · Issue #1449 · cloudflare/cloudflared","url":"https://github.com/cloudflare/cloudflared/issues/1449"},{"title":"Cloudflare Tunnel with Websocket - Cloudflare Tunnel - Cloudflare Community","url":"https://community.cloudflare.com/t/cloudflare-tunnel-with-websocket/387212"},{"title":"Cloudflared Tunnel very flakey with websockets - Cloudflare Tunnel - Cloudflare Community","url":"https://community.cloudflare.com/t/cloudflared-tunnel-very-flakey-with-websockets/387565"},{"title":"WebSockets · Cloudflare Workers docs","url":"https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/runtime-apis/websockets/"},{"title":"Use WebSockets · Cloudflare Durable Objects docs","url":"https://developers.cloudflare.com/durable-objects/best-practices/websockets/"},{"title":"Websocket, Cloudflare tunnel, apache httpd and a bit of security | by Lorenzo | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@lorenzo_33729/websocket-cloudflare-tunnel-apache-httpd-and-a-bit-of-security-dffd6583090c"},{"title":"Websocket connections not working through Cloudflare Tunnels - Access - Cloudflare Community","url":"https://community.cloudflare.com/t/websocket-connections-not-working-through-cloudflare-tunnels/604188"},{"title":"HTTP/2 Protocol Implementation | cloudflare/cloudflared | DeepWiki","url":"https://deepwiki.com/cloudflare/cloudflared/4.2-http2-protocol-implementation"},{"title":"Common errors · Cloudflare One docs","url":"https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/networks/connectors/cloudflare-tunnel/troubleshoot-tunnels/common-errors/"}]

Here are the search results for your query about cloudflared tunnel, WebSocket frames, and server-sent events:

## Key Findings:

**Server-Sent Events Buffering:**
When using GET requests, none of the events are delivered to the client until the server finishes sending all events and closes the connection, at which point the entire stream is flushed to the client all at once. Proxied traffic through Cloudflare Tunnel is buffered by default unless the origin server includes the Content-Type: text/event-stream response header, which tells cloudflared to stream data as it arrives instead of buffering the entire response.

**WebSocket Frame Handling:**
An incoming binary frame is fully buffered before the message event fires, regardless of binaryType. The writer flushes after each write for streaming connections, with WebSocket, TCP, and control stream types always flushing, and HTTP responses flushing if they have streaming content like SSE, gRPC, chunked encoding, or no content-length.

**Performance Optimization:**
Batching 10-100 logical messages into a single WebSocket frame reduces context switches proportionally, with recommendations to batch every 50-100ms or every 50-100 messages, whichever comes first.

**Known Issues:**
The delayed flush behavior happens after the origin, likely in the tunnel or edge.

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about OpenAI Codex CLI authentication and ChatGPT subscription login:

## Authentication Methods

When setting up the Codex CLI, you can choose OpenAI authentication using ChatGPT or an API key. Features that rely on ChatGPT credits, such as fast mode, are available only when you sign in with ChatGPT. If you sign in with an API key, Codex uses standard API pricing instead.

## CLI Login Process

To authenticate the Codex CLI with a ChatGPT account or API key, Codex opens a browser for the ChatGPT OAuth flow with no flags. You can also use the OAuth device code flow instead of launching a browser window.

## Subscription Requirements

Codex is included with ChatGPT Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise/Edu plans. For a limited time Codex is included with ChatGPT Free and Go, and all other plans enjoy 2x rate limits.

## Session Management

When you sign in to the Codex app, CLI, or IDE Extension using either ChatGPT or an API key, Codex caches your login details and reuses them the next time you start the CLI or extension. The CLI and extension share the same cached login details. For sign in with ChatGPT sessions, Codex refreshes tokens automatically during use before they expire, so active sessions usually continue without requiring another browser login.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Codex CLI exec mode with JSON output streaming in non-interactive/headless environments:

## JSON Output in Non-Interactive Mode

When you enable --json, stdout becomes a JSON Lines (JSONL) stream so you can capture every event Codex emits while it's running. Event types include thread.started, turn.started, turn.completed, turn.failed, item.* , and error. Item types include agent messages, reasoning, command executions, file changes, MCP tool calls, web searches, and plan updates.

## Basic Usage

While codex exec runs, Codex streams progress to stderr and prints only the final agent message to stdout. To capture the full event stream, use the `--json` flag:

```bash
codex exec --json "your prompt here"
```

Codex writes formatted output by default. Add --json to receive newline-delimited JSON events (one per state change).

## Headless Execution Features

The headless execution mode (codex exec) is a non-interactive command-line interface for running Codex programmatically or in automation workflows. Unlike the interactive TUI, exec mode runs a single session to completion without user interaction, emits events to stdout/stderr, and exits when the agent determines the task is complete.

## Important Behavior with Output Files

When --json and -o are combined, stdout gets the full JSONL event stream, but -o writes only the final plain-text message (not JSONL). The -o flag always captures the last agent message as text regardless of --json.

This makes Codex CLI suitable for automation, CI/CD pipelines, and programmatic orchestration of agent tasks in non-interactive environments.

Based on the search results, here are the key findings regarding ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription API access via OAuth tokens:

## Traditional API Access

You cannot use your Plus subscription to make API calls, integrate ChatGPT into third-party applications, or access models programmatically—those capabilities require separate API credentials and billing. ChatGPT Plus subscription provides enhanced access to the web interface but does not include API access.

## OAuth Token Exchange (Emerging Feature)

However, as of 2026, there are new developments enabling OAuth-based programmatic access. Some tools are implementing OAuth token exchanges that convert ChatGPT Plus/Pro credentials into API keys for programmatic use. A March 2026 update allows logging in with your ChatGPT Plus account to access GPT-5.4 directly without separate OpenAI API billing.

## Implementation Details

ChatGPT Pro/Max subscription models can be accessed through tools like LiteLLM using OAuth device flow authentication, with ChatGPT subscription access being native to the Responses API. Users can run OAuth login flows with their ChatGPT Plus account, and the OAuth token is stored locally and used to call GPT-5.4 via the Codex endpoint without separate API billing.

## Important Notes

Consumer subscriptions (Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise) and API usage operate on completely separate billing systems. This OAuth approach represents a recent shift toward allowing Plus/Pro subscribers to leverage their existing subscriptions for programmatic access through third-party tools and emerging integrations.

Based on the search results, here's information about OpenClaw's OpenAI community CLI subscription authentication:

## OpenAI Subscription OAuth Support

OpenAI explicitly supports subscription OAuth usage in external tools and workflows like OpenClaw. OpenAI Codex OAuth is explicitly supported for use in external tools like OpenClaw.

## Authentication Methods

For agent models using openai/* models through the Codex runtime, you can sign in with Codex auth for ChatGPT/Codex subscription use, or configure a Codex-compatible OpenAI API-key backup when you intentionally want API-key auth.

## Setting Up OpenAI OAuth

The CLI command for authentication is: openclaw models auth login --provider openai-codex

For the onboarding process, you log into your OpenAI/ChatGPT account in the browser and click Continue to authorize OpenClaw to use your subscription, then copy the full URL from your browser's address bar (including the ?code=<REDACTED> part) and paste it back into your terminal.

## Subscription Coverage

Your ChatGPT Plus ($20/month) or ChatGPT Pro ($200/month) subscription covers Codex usage inside OpenClaw at a flat rate, with no per-token API billing.

## Token Management

OAuth tokens from ChatGPT refresh automatically during active use.
